Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[daimyō]] family shrine for the [[Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|ja|八戸三社大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|qq=}}, a festival recognized as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} ([[Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * {{ill|Gassan Shrine|ja|月山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gassan Shrine|qq=}} ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=}} and Kyoto's {{ill|Shōgo-in|ja|聖護院|1=Shōgo-in|lt=Shōgo-in Temple|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-in|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-in|qq=}}, returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|ja|八戸三社大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|qq=}} === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachinohe City shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[en:Ogami Shrine]]

It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachinohe City shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[en:Ogami Shrine]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:八戸藩]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōkami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:おがみ神社.jpg|240px|Ōkami Shrine]] |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]] 2-1-51 |coordinates = {{Coord|40|30|53.9|N|141|29|25.4|E|region:JP-02_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Takaokami no Kami]]
Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[daimyō]] family shrine for the [[Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} ([[Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachinohe City shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[en:Ogami Shrine]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:八戸藩]] [[Category:青森県の旧村社]]

It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachinohe City shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain�]] [[Category:青森県の旧村社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōkami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:おがみ神社.jpg|240px|Ōkami Shrine]] |location = [[Aomori Prefecture]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]] 2-1-51 |coordinates = {{Coord|40|30|53.9|N|141|29|25.4|E|region:JP-02_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Takaokami no Kami]]
Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[daimyō]] family shrine for the [[Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} ([[Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hachinohe City shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:青森県の旧村社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:青森県の旧村社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:青森県の旧村社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[Meiji period]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:八戸市の神社]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11261436]] Wikidata name: Ogami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōkami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:おがみ神社.jpg|240px|Ōkami Shrine]] |location = [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]] 2-1-51 |coordinates = {{Coord|40|30|53.9|N|141|29|25.4|E|region:JP-02_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Takaokami no Kami]]
Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=|1=Shinkawa Shrine (Ise)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chojasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}}

It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11261436]] Wikidata name: Ogami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōkami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:おがみ神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]] 2-1-51 |coordinates = {{Coord|40.514972|N|141.490389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Takaokami no Kami]]
Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|qid=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|qid=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-Hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Ogami Shrine]] [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}}

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|qq=|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}}
