It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Horeizan Ogami-jinja]][[ja:龗神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11261436]] Wikidata name: Ogami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōkami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:おがみ神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]] 2-1-51 |coordinates = {{Coord|40.514972|N|141.490389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Takaokami no Kami]]
Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}}

Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Horeizan Ogami-jinja]][[ja:龗神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11261436]] Wikidata name: Ogami Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōkami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:おがみ神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]] 2-1-51 |coordinates = {{Coord|40.514972|N|141.490389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Takaokami no Kami]]
Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:龗神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:龗神社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for [[:en:Hachinohe Castle|Hachinohe Castle]] and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]]. The shrine is also the origin of the [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]], a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible cultural heritage]] list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ōjin Tennō]]) * [[:en:Gassan Shrine|Gassan Shrine]] ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's [[:en:Shōgo-in|Shōgo-in Temple]], returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the [[:en:Morioka Domain|Morioka Domain]] and later designated as the guardian shrine of [[:en:Hachinohe Domain|Hachinohe Domain]], receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' [[:en:Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai]] === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:龗神社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for {{ill|Hachinohe Castle|en|Hachinohe Castle|WD=Q994058|lt=Hachinohe Castle}} and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|WD=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan}}. The shrine is also the origin of the {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}}, a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=Intangible cultural heritage}} list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōjin Tennō}}) * {{ill|Gassan Shrine|en|Gassan Shrine|WD=Q11516052|lt=Gassan Shrine}} ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's {{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in Temple}}, returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Morioka Domain|en|Morioka Domain|WD=Q908854|lt=Morioka Domain}} and later designated as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Hachinohe Domain|en|Hachinohe Domain|WD=Q7884927|lt=Hachinohe Domain}}, receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龗神社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for {{ill|Hachinohe Castle|en|Hachinohe Castle|WD=Q994058|lt=Hachinohe Castle}} and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|WD=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan}}. The shrine is also the origin of the {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}}, a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=Intangible cultural heritage}} list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōjin Tennō}}) * {{ill|Gassan Shrine|en|Gassan Shrine|WD=Q11516052|lt=Gassan Shrine}} ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's {{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in Temple}}, returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Morioka Domain|en|Morioka Domain|WD=Q908854|lt=Morioka Domain}} and later designated as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Hachinohe Domain|en|Hachinohe Domain|WD=Q7884927|lt=Hachinohe Domain}}, receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龗神社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850|1=Uchimaru}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|1=Kuraokami}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for {{ill|Hachinohe Castle|en|Hachinohe Castle|WD=Q994058|lt=Hachinohe Castle|1=Hachinohe Castle}} and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|WD=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan|1=Nanbu clan}}. The shrine is also the origin of the {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|1=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}}, a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=Intangible cultural heritage|1=Intangible cultural heritage}} list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōjin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|Gassan Shrine|en|Gassan Shrine|WD=Q11516052|lt=Gassan Shrine|1=Gassan Shrine}} ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Three Shrines|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's {{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in Temple|1=Shōgo-in}}, returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Morioka Domain|en|Morioka Domain|WD=Q908854|lt=Morioka Domain|1=Morioka Domain}} and later designated as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Hachinohe Domain|en|Hachinohe Domain|WD=Q7884927|lt=Hachinohe Domain|1=Hachinohe Domain}}, receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|1=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龗神社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Hōryō Daimyōjin |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Hōryō Shrine |Festivals = August 2 / September 2 |Rituals = Hōryō Kagura Festival (mid-May)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31 – August 4, September 2)
Hōryō Kagura Year-End Festival (November 16) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=25.4|label=Ōkami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|location = {{ill|Uchimaru|WD=Q11393850|1=Uchimaru}} |deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|1=Kuraokami}} }} '''Ōkami Shrine''' (龗神社, Ogami-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Uchimaru (Hachinohe)|Uchimaru]], [[Hachinohe City]], {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}. As the oldest shrine in Hachinohe, its recorded history dates back to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, approximately 1,000 years ago, with its origins believed to predate even earlier. Officially called Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine (法霊山龗神社), it is also colloquially known as Hōryō Shrine or Hōryōzan. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the tutelary shrine for {{ill|Hachinohe Castle|en|Hachinohe Castle|WD=Q994058|lt=Hachinohe Castle|1=Hachinohe Castle}} and was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] family shrine for the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|WD=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan|1=Nanbu clan}}. The shrine is also the origin of the {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|1=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}}, a festival recognized as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|WD=Q84036549|lt=Intangible cultural heritage|1=Intangible cultural heritage}} list. The kanji for "Ōkami" is rare, combining the radical for "rain" (雨) over three horizontal "mouths" (口) and a "dragon" (龍) below. == Deities == ; Main Deities * [[Kuraokami|Takaokami no Kami]] * Hōryō Daimyōjin * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮 (八戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōjin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|Gassan Shrine|en|Gassan Shrine|WD=Q11516052|lt=Gassan Shrine|1=Gassan Shrine}} ([[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi no Mikoto]]) * {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} ([[Seoritsu-hime|Seoritsu Hime]]) == History == The origins of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine are thought to trace back to the [[:en:Ubusuna|Ubusuna]] (tutelary deity) worship of the Kashiwazaki region, which encompasses the modern Hachinohe city center. The shrine also served as the guardian of the Kashiwazaki Reservoir, crucial for local agriculture. Scholars debate the exact date of establishment, with some suggesting the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} based on the spread of ubusuna worship, while others propose the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} when the concept of shrines became more formalized in the region. However, historical records confirm the shrine's existence from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} onward{{Cite web|url=http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/c/history/ |title=Official History of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine|accessdate=2015-11}}. The shrine gained prominence during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} with the establishment of its connection to a Shugendō practitioner named Hōryō, who, after extensive training at {{ill|Kumano Shrines|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Three Shrines|WD=Q10403700}} and Kyoto's {{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|WD=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in Temple|1=Shōgo-in}}, returned to Hachinohe. Local legends recount Hōryō sacrificing himself by leaping into a sacred pond to summon rain during a severe drought, leading to his deification as Hōryō Daimyōjin. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Ōkami Shrine was reconstructed by the {{ill|Morioka Domain|en|Morioka Domain|WD=Q908854|lt=Morioka Domain|1=Morioka Domain}} and later designated as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Hachinohe Domain|en|Hachinohe Domain|WD=Q7884927|lt=Hachinohe Domain|1=Hachinohe Domain}}, receiving lands and donations from the Nanbu clan. == Shrine Grounds == The current main hall dates to approximately 190 years ago when it was relocated to its present site. The structure features a unique layout, with a corridor connecting the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall, forming a [[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style. == Festivals == === Hachinohe Sansha Taisai === {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} This festival began in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 6 (1721) with a procession of a portable shrine from Ōkami Shrine to {{ill|Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|長者山新羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}}. It was expanded in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} to include other shrines and is now recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. === Hōryō Kagura Festival === Held in mid-May, this festival features performances of {{ill|Hōryō Kagura|ja|法霊神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryō Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional dance rooted in Shugendō practices, with vibrant costumes and symbolic choreography. == Cultural Properties == === National Designations === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|WD=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|1=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} === Prefectural Designations === * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ''' Hōryō Kagura (designated in 1986) === Other Cultural Assets === * Ancient mirrors and swords * Sacred portable shrines from the Edo period == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.ogami-jinja.jp/ Official website of Hōryōzan Ōkami Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/03.htm Ōkami Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|龗神社|version=104299390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龗神社]] {{Commons category|Horeizan Ogami-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11261436}} [[Category:Hachinohe Domain]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
